headline: All six crew members killed in KC-135 refueling plane crash in Iraq, U.S. military says
summary: The KC-135 is used to refuel other aircraft in mid-air. This is the fourth reported aircraft loss since the Iran war started.

headline: A Look At GE Vernova (GEV) Valuation After AI Driven Upgrades And US$150b Backlog
summary: GE Vernova (GEV) has been in focus after a wave of analyst upgrades linked to rising gas turbine demand from AI data centers and power projects, alongside a reported US$150b order backlog. See our latest analysis for GE Vernova. Those backlog headlines have played out in the share price too, with GE Vernova’s 1-day share price return of a 1.8% decline sitting against a 90-day share price return of 23.9% and a 1-year total shareholder return of about 1.8x, which points to strong momentum...

headline: GE Aerospace Deepens Palantir AI Use In Defense And Factory Operations
summary: GE Aerospace, part of General Electric (NYSE:GE), has expanded a multi year partnership with Palantir Technologies to roll out advanced AI solutions across the U.S. Air Force. The partnership now covers mission readiness, sustainment and new engine production systems, with a focus on supply chain and maintenance automation. AI agents are being used to support decision making in aircraft sustainment and to streamline GE Aerospace's own production processes. For you as an investor, this...

headline: GE Aerospace and Palantir Expand Partnership to Transform Military Aircraft Readiness with AI
summary: MIAMI & AURORA, Colo., March 12, 2026--GE Aerospace (NYSE:GE) and Palantir Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ: PLTR) today announced a multi-year partnership expansion to accelerate the transformation of military aviation readiness for the U.S. Air Force and operations across GE Aerospace’s production system. Together, the companies are deploying advanced agentic AI-powered solutions to ensure GE can maximize production and aircraft remain mission ready.

Together, the companies are deploying advanced agentic AI-powered solutions to ensure GE can maximize production and aircraft remain mission ready.
